reasoning: The FRED statistical model provides a short-term probability estimate of P=0.35 for the spot rate to increase above the baseline of ~7.283. This is driven by a broad downward trend observed since mid-January, combined with historical seasonality for this time of year. While some analysts project USD/CNY to rise to 7.4-7.5 over the next year due to potential US tariffs, the PBOC has a strong track record of strictly managing and defending the 7.30-7.35 ceiling. Concurrently, anticipated global easing by the US Fed may exert broader downward pressure on the USD. Following the principle that exchange rates approximate a random walk over the long term, and adhering strictly to the prompt's instruction that probabilities for more distant horizons must move closer to 0.5, I start near the FRED-recommended 0.36 for the immediate 1-week horizon and progressively increase uncertainty so that the probability smoothly approaches 0.49 at the 1-year mark.
